title: Review & Setlist: Lucinda Williams brings protest songs, fighting spirit to Medford
author: Terence Cawley
bullets: Lucinda Williams performed at the Chevalier Theatre in Medford, focusing on protest songs from her recent albums.
Unable to play guitar due to a 2020 stroke, Williams performed with strong vocals and engaged in political commentary.
The setlist included covers of Bob Marley and Neil Young, with a closing message emphasizing music's power as a weapon.

title: Twins unload on Justin Slaten as Red Sox lose 8-6 at Fenway Park
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Justin Slaten gave up four hits and four runs, including two home runs, in the seventh inning against the Twins.
Red Sox manager Chad Tracy defended pulling starter Payton Tolle after six innings and 85 pitches.
Despite a strong offensive effort, Boston fell 8-6 to the Twins, leaving runners stranded in the eighth inning.

title: Roger Clemens throws out first pitch at Fenway Park to son, Twins player Kody Clemens
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roger Clemens, 63, threw the ceremonial first pitch at Fenway Park before the Boston Red Sox faced the Minnesota Twins.
Clemens tossed the pitch to his son, Kody Clemens, a Twins player in his second MLB season at age 30.
Red Sox distributed Roger Clemens bobbleheads commemorating his 20-strikeout games, coinciding with his appearance at Fenway.

title: Celtics’ Derrick White named to 2025-26 NBA All-Defensive First Team
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Derrick White of the Boston Celtics named to 2025-26 NBA All-Defensive First Team with 58 votes.
White recorded 98 blocks and 88 steals, leading all guards with 552 contested shots and 1.3 blocks per game.
Celtics ended second in Eastern Conference with 56-26 record, despite first-round exit to Philadelphia 76ers.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: "Glory" (1989) is streaming on Netflix until June 1. It stars Matthew Broderick and Denzel Washington in an Oscar-winning role.
New animated film "Arco" with an English dub cast including Mark Ruffalo is available on Hulu.
"Maximum Pleasure Guaranteed" features Tatiana Maslany as a newly divorced mom. It's streaming on Apple TV.

title: Chad Tracy provides injury updates for Garrett Crochet, Roman Anthony
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Garrett Crochet is set for a throwing session and live batting practice, key steps towards his return.
Roman Anthony is struggling with pain management and remains on the 10-day IL since May 4.
Trevor Story will miss 6-10 weeks following sports hernia surgery, per Red Sox manager Chad Tracy.

title: Sam Kennedy says Red Sox looking to add bat ‘earlier than ever’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox President Sam Kennedy confirmed early talks to acquire a hitter to improve their struggling offense.
Boston's offensive struggles include ranking near last in MLB for runs (181) and home runs (36) through 49 games.
Despite a recent win streak, injuries, and poor performances, Red Sox explore trades ahead of the August 3 deadline.

title: Mock draft predicts Red Sox will select Bishop Feehan star in first round
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: MLB.com's Jonathan Mayo predicts Red Sox will draft Bishop Feehan's Brody Bumila at No. 20 in his mock draft.
Bumila, a 6-foot-9 left-hander, showcased high-speed fastballs and a mix of changeups and sliders in recent games.
Despite concerns over previous injuries, Bumila's potential as a left-handed pitcher has drawn significant scout interest.

title: Red Sox’ Trevor Story undergoes sports hernia surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox shortstop Trevor Story undergoes successful hernia surgery and could miss 6-10 weeks.
Story has faced multiple injuries since joining the Red Sox in 2022, impacting his performance.
Isaiah Kiner-Falefa, Andruw Monasterio, and Nick Sogard to cover shortstop in Story's absence.

title: Peter Wolf left one Boston art school to form the J. Geils Band. Six decades later, he earned an honorary doctorate from another.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Peter Wolf received an honorary doctorate from Massachusetts College of Art and Design, six decades after leaving art school.
Wolf, known for leading the J. Geils Band, started as an art student without a high school diploma.
Despite not graduating, Wolf pursued painting and music, leading to a legendary career and multiple Rock Hall nominations.
